steps.title: Isolate the variable term
steps.content:
Start with the given equation and subtract $7c$ from both sides. This leaves the expression involving $b$ alone on the left side.
$$b^2+7c=4d$$
$$b^2=4d-7c$$

steps.title: Check the radical
steps.content:
The condition $d>\frac{7}{4}c$ makes the quantity under the radical positive.
$$4d>7c$$
$$4d-7c>0$$
So real values of $b$ are possible.

steps.title: Use both signs
steps.content:
A number whose square is positive can be either positive or negative, so include both signs when solving for $b$.
$$b=\pm\sqrt{4d-7c}$$
The answer is C.
